name: arbitration + payment
contract: Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration in Beijing. Payment by T/T 60 days after delivery.
reference: Two material risks. (1) Arbitration is seated in Beijing, a venue unfavorable to the Vietnamese SME; a neutral seat such as SIAC (Singapore) or HKIAC should be proposed. (2) Payment by T/T deferred 60 days creates non-payment risk and should be secured by an irrevocable L/C with any advance capped.

name: inspection at destination
contract: Quality inspection shall take place at the destination port upon arrival.
reference: One material risk: quality inspection at the destination port exposes the seller to transit-damage disputes. Inspection should instead occur at the port of loading (FOB) by an independent inspector such as SGS or Bureau Veritas.

name: advance + inspection
contract: Buyer requires 50% advance payment. Inspection at destination port.
reference: Two material risks. (1) A 50% advance payment is high and should be capped (around 30%) or secured. (2) Inspection at the destination port should be moved to the port of loading (FOB) under an independent inspector.

name: all three
contract: Arbitration in Beijing. T/T payment after 30 days. Inspection at destination port.
reference: Three material risks. (1) Arbitration seated in Beijing is unfavorable; propose a neutral seat (SIAC/HKIAC). (2) T/T payment deferred 30 days risks non-payment; secure with an L/C. (3) Inspection at the destination port should move to the port of loading (FOB).

name: clean contract
contract: Both parties cooperate in good faith with standard delivery terms.
reference: No material risks to flag. The contract contains only standard good-faith cooperation and delivery terms acceptable to the Vietnamese SME.
